title: Straight Leg Raise
instructions: Lie on your back with one knee bent and the other leg straight. Tighten the thigh muscle of the straight leg and slowly raise it to the height of the opposite knee. Hold for 3 seconds, then lower slowly. Keep your lower back flat on the floor throughout.

title: Quad Sets
instructions: Sit or lie with your leg straight. Tighten the muscle on the front of your thigh by pushing the back of your knee down into the floor. Hold the contraction for 5 seconds, then relax. You should see your kneecap move slightly upward when contracting correctly.

title: Standing Hamstring Curl
instructions: Stand holding a stable surface for balance. Slowly bend one knee, bringing your heel toward your buttock. Hold for 2 seconds at the top, then lower slowly. Keep your thighs parallel and your trunk upright throughout the movement.

title: Mini Wall Squats
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall and feet shoulder-width apart, about 30 cm from the wall. Slowly slide down until your knees are bent to approximately 45 degrees. Hold for 5 seconds, then slide back up. Do not let your knees travel beyond your toes.

title: Ankle Pumps
instructions: Sit or lie with your leg elevated. Slowly pull your toes toward you as far as comfortable, then push them away from you. Move through the full available range in a smooth, controlled rhythm. This exercise also helps circulation.

title: Heel Raises
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a stable surface for balance. Slowly rise onto your toes, lifting your heels as high as comfortable. Hold for 2 seconds at the top, then lower slowly. Keep your weight evenly distributed across all toes.

title: Ankle Alphabet
instructions: Sit with your leg supported and foot hanging free. Using your big toe as a pointer, trace each letter of the alphabet in the air. Move only your ankle — keep your leg still. Perform the entire alphabet with each foot.

title: Pendulum Exercises
instructions: Lean forward at the waist, supporting yourself with your unaffected arm on a table. Let the affected arm hang straight down. Gently swing the arm in small circles, forward and back, and side to side. Let gravity and momentum do the work — keep the arm relaxed.

title: Wall Slides — Shoulder Flexion
instructions: Stand facing a wall. Place both hands on the wall at chest height. Slowly walk your fingers up the wall, sliding your hands upward as far as comfortable. Hold the top position for 5 seconds, then slowly walk your fingers back down. Keep your body close to the wall.

title: Shoulder External Rotation with Band
instructions: Stand with your elbow bent at 90 degrees and tucked against your side, holding a resistance band attached to a fixed point at elbow height. Rotate your forearm outward away from your body, keeping your elbow pinned to your side. Slowly return to the start position.

title: Clamshells
instructions: Lie on your side with hips and knees bent to approximately 45 degrees, feet together. Keeping your feet touching, raise your top knee as far as you can without moving your pelvis. Hold for 2 seconds, then lower slowly. You should feel this in the outside of your hip.

title: Supine Hip Flexor Stretch
instructions: Lie on your back at the edge of a bed so that one leg hangs off the side. Pull the opposite knee toward your chest and hold it with both hands. Let the hanging leg relax downward — you should feel a stretch in the front of that hip. Hold for 30 seconds.

title: Cat–Cow Stretch
instructions: Start on your hands and knees with a neutral spine. For the cow position, let your stomach drop toward the floor and gently arch your back, lifting your head and tailbone. For the cat position, round your back toward the ceiling, tucking your chin and tailbone. Alternate slowly between positions.

title: Bird–Dog
instructions: Start on your hands and knees with a neutral spine. Slowly extend your right arm forward and your left leg backward until both are parallel to the floor. Hold for 5 seconds, keeping your hips level and your core engaged. Return to the start and repeat on the opposite side.
